notes.auth_setup: All authenticated requests require Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header. JWT obtained via POST /auth/login.
notes.company_scoping: Audit log endpoints are company-scoped via the company-context middleware (x-company-id header or JWT companyId claim). Dead-letter queue endpoints are NOT company-scoped (infrastructure-level).
notes.data_seeding: Audit log records are created via event bus emission, not via direct API. Tests must seed data by emitting events or inserting directly into the audit_logs table. Dead-letter entries are created by event handler failures; mock BullMQ for test control.
notes.permission_setup: Tests require users with specific access group permissions: system.audit-log.list (view) for audit endpoints, system.dead-letter-queue.list (view/edit) for DLQ endpoints.
notes.response_envelope: All responses follow the envelope pattern: { success: true, data: T, meta?: { cursor, hasMore, total } } for success, { success: false, error: { code, message } } for errors.
notes.story_coverage: E3.1 has no API endpoints (internal event bus infrastructure). E3.2 covers audit-log routes. E3.3 covers dead-letter-queue routes.
notes.ordering: Tests ordered: reads first (GET endpoints), then mutations (POST reprocess). No direct create or delete API endpoints exist in E3.

endpoints.description: Query audit log with filters and cursor pagination (E3.2, AC#3)

test_cases.name: Audit log list - happy path with default params
Audit log list - filter by entityType

body_assertions: every record in data has entityType === 'User'

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs WHERE company_id = '{{companyId}}' AND entity_type = 'User'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - filter by entityId

body_assertions: every record in data has entityId === '{{known_user_id}}'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - filter by action (CREATE)

body_assertions: every record in data has action === 'CREATE'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - filter by userId

body_assertions: every record in data has userId === '{{known_user_id}}'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - filter by date range (dateFrom + dateTo)

body_assertions: every record timestamp is within the specified date range

test_cases.name: Audit log list - multiple filters combined

body_assertions: every record has entityType === 'AccessGroup' AND action === 'CREATE'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - cursor pagination (page 1 then page 2)

body_assertions: page 1: data.length === 2, meta.hasMore === true, meta.cursor is non-null UUID
page 2 (using cursor from page 1): returns next set of records
page 2 records do not overlap with page 1 records
records are ordered by timestamp descending

test_cases.name: Audit log list - descending timestamp order (most recent first)

body_assertions: for each consecutive pair of records: data[i].timestamp >= data[i+1].timestamp

test_cases.name: Audit log list - empty result (no matching records)
Audit log list - company isolation (no cross-company data leakage)

body_assertions: response.body.data contains ONLY records where companyId === company_b_id
no records from company_a appear in results

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs WHERE company_id = '{{company_b_id}}'

test_cases.name: Audit log list - records older than 6 years still accessible (NFR40)

body_assertions: pre-seeded 7-year-old audit record appears in results
old records have all fields intact (entityType, entityId, action, userId, etc.)

preconditions: INSERT audit_log record with timestamp 7 years in the past before running this test

db_verification.query: SELECT * FROM audit_logs WHERE company_id = '{{companyId}}' AND timestamp < '2020-01-01'
db_verification.expected: at least 1 record exists and is returned by the API

test_cases.name: Audit log list - invalid action enum value returns 400

body_assertions: error message references invalid action value

test_cases.name: Audit log list - invalid entityId (not UUID) returns 400

body_assertions: error message references invalid UUID format

test_cases.name: Audit log list - invalid userId (not UUID) returns 400

body_assertions: error message references invalid UUID format

test_cases.name: Audit log list - invalid dateFrom format returns 400

body_assertions: error message references invalid ISO datetime format

test_cases.name: Audit log list - limit exceeds max (>200) returns 400
Audit log list - limit below minimum (<1) returns 400
Audit log list - unauthenticated request returns 401
Audit log list - user without system.audit-log.list permission returns 403
Audit log list - response includes all expected fields per record

body_assertions: each record has: id (uuid), companyId (uuid), entityType (string), entityId (string), action (enum), beforeData (object|null), afterData (object|null), userId (string), isAiAction (boolean), aiConfidence (number|null), correlationId (string|null), timestamp (ISO datetime)

endpoints.description: Full change history for a specific entity in chronological order (E3.2, AC#4)

test_cases.name: Entity history - happy path returns full change history

body_assertions: every record has entityType === 'AccessGroup' AND entityId === '{{known_access_group_id}}'
meta.total matches number of audit records for this entity

db_verification.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_logs WHERE company_id = '{{companyId}}' AND entity_type = 'AccessGroup' AND entity_id = '{{known_access_group_id}}'

test_cases.name: Entity history - chronological ascending order

body_assertions: for each consecutive pair: data[i].timestamp <= data[i+1].timestamp (ascending chronological order)
earliest event (e.g. LOGIN) appears first, most recent last

test_cases.name: Entity history - cursor pagination for large histories

body_assertions: if hasMore, cursor is a valid UUID
page 2 using cursor returns subsequent records with no overlap

test_cases.name: Entity history - no records found returns 200 with empty array
Entity history - company isolation (no cross-company data)

body_assertions: response.body.data is empty array [] (company B user cannot see company A audit records)

test_cases.name: Entity history - invalid entityId (not UUID) returns 400

body_assertions: error references invalid UUID format for entityId

test_cases.name: Entity history - empty entityType returns 400

body_assertions: error references entityType min length validation (min 1 char)

test_cases.name: Entity history - unauthenticated request returns 401
Entity history - user without permission returns 403

endpoints.description: List dead-letter queue entries with optional filtering and pagination (E3.3, AC#3)

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - happy path with default params
Dead letter queue list - response includes all expected fields per entry

body_assertions: each entry has: id (string), eventName (string), payload (any), error (string), retryCount (number), originalTimestamp (ISO string), createdAt (ISO string), reprocessed (boolean)

preconditions: At least one DLQ entry exists (seed via failed event handler)

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - filter by eventName

body_assertions: every entry in data has eventName === 'user.login'

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - filter by reprocessed=false

body_assertions: every entry in data has reprocessed === false

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - filter by reprocessed=true

body_assertions: every entry in data has reprocessed === true
entries have reprocessedAt set to ISO timestamp

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - cursor pagination

body_assertions: if hasMore, cursor is non-null string (BullMQ job ID)
page 2 using cursor returns next entries with no overlap

preconditions: At least 3 DLQ entries seeded for pagination testing

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - empty result (no entries)
Dead letter queue list - Redis unavailable returns 503
preconditions: deadLetterService must be null on the Fastify instance (simulate Redis unavailable)

test_cases.name: Dead letter queue list - invalid limit (>100) returns 400
Dead letter queue list - invalid limit (<1) returns 400
Dead letter queue list - unauthenticated returns 401
Dead letter queue list - user without permission returns 403

endpoints.description: Re-emit a failed event from the dead-letter queue and mark it as reprocessed (E3.3, AC#4)

test_cases.name: Reprocess DLQ entry - happy path

db_verification.query: Verify via BullMQ: Job.fromId(queue, dlq_entry_id).data.reprocessed === true AND .data.reprocessedAt is ISO timestamp
db_verification.expected: DLQ entry marked as reprocessed with timestamp

side_effects: The original event is re-emitted through the event bus
All subscribers for that event type receive the payload
If audit mapping exists, a new AuditLog record is created

test_cases.name: Reprocess DLQ entry - verify event re-emission

db_verification.query: Check that a new audit_log record was created by the re-emitted event (if the event has an audit mapping): SELECT * FROM audit_logs WHERE company_id = '{{companyId}}' ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 1
db_verification.expected: Most recent audit_log record matches the re-emitted event's entityType and action

test_cases.name: Reprocess DLQ entry - entry not found returns 404
Reprocess DLQ entry - already reprocessed returns 409
preconditions: DLQ entry must exist with reprocessed === true

test_cases.name: Reprocess DLQ entry - Redis unavailable returns 503
preconditions: deadLetterService must be null on the Fastify instance

test_cases.name: Reprocess DLQ entry - unauthenticated returns 401
Reprocess DLQ entry - user without edit permission returns 403

body_assertions: user has view but not edit permission for system.dead-letter-queue.list

test_data_setup.description: Required test data seeding before running the test plan

users.description: JWT for admin user with FULL_ACCESS access group (has system.audit-log.list view + system.dead-letter-queue.list view/edit)
JWT for user without system.audit-log.list or system.dead-letter-queue.list permissions
JWT for user in a different company (company B) for cross-company isolation tests
JWT for user with system.dead-letter-queue.list view but NOT edit permission

audit_log_seeding: Create multiple audit log records across different entityTypes (User, AccessGroup, UserAccessGroup, CompanyProfile)
Create audit records with different actions (CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, LOGIN)
Create audit records with different timestamps for date range testing
Create at least 1 record with timestamp 7+ years ago for retention test (AL-012)
Create audit records in 2+ different companies for company isolation testing
Create at least 5+ records for the same entity for pagination testing
dead_letter_seeding: Seed 3+ DLQ entries via failed event handler simulation or direct BullMQ job insertion
At least 1 entry with reprocessed=true and reprocessedAt set
Entries with different eventName values for filter testing

variables.{{companyId}}: UUID of the primary test company
variables.{{company_b_id}}: UUID of a secondary test company
variables.{{known_user_id}}: UUID of a user with multiple audit log entries
variables.{{known_access_group_id}}: UUID of an access group with CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE audit trail
variables.{{company_a_group_id}}: UUID of an access group belonging to company A (for isolation testing)
variables.{{dlq_entry_id}}: BullMQ job ID of an unprocessed DLQ entry
variables.{{already_reprocessed_dlq_id}}: BullMQ job ID of a DLQ entry already marked reprocessed
variables.{{valid_uuid}}: Any valid UUID v4 for param validation testing

coverage_notes.e3_1_event_bus: E3.1 (Event Bus Infrastructure) has no API endpoints — covered by unit tests in event-bus.test.ts and event-bus.plugin.test.ts
coverage_notes.audit_immutability: AuditLog immutability (PostgreSQL RULE-based UPDATE/DELETE prevention) is tested at the DB level in packages/db/src/__tests__/audit-immutability.test.ts, not via API tests
coverage_notes.audit_creation: AuditLog records are created via event bus emission (not a direct API endpoint). The audit service subscribes to business events and writes records automatically. Test seeding must use this flow or direct DB insertion.
coverage_notes.retry_mechanism: E3.3 retry + exponential backoff is tested in event-bus-retry.test.ts and retry-handler.test.ts (unit/integration tests), not via API endpoints
coverage_notes.idempotency_guard: E3.3 IdempotencyGuard utility is tested in idempotency.test.ts (unit tests), not via API endpoints
